title: N-body Lie Algebra Dimension Scaling Formulas

L3.expression: unknown (only one data point: N=3 gives 116)
L3.needed: N=4 or N=5 level 3 data to determine formula

observations.degree_pattern: L0 ~ N^2, L1 ~ N^2, L2 ~ N^3. Leading polynomial degree appears to increase with level, suggesting the algebra dimension grows as N^(level+1) or faster.
observations.leading_coefficients: L0: 1/2, L1: 3/2, L2: 13/6. The numerator sequence {1, 3, 13} does not follow an obvious pattern yet.
observations.super_polynomial_growth: For fixed N, the growth across levels is super-polynomial in the level: L3/L2 = 6.82 for N=3, which is much larger than L2/L1 = 2.83.
